question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: prune()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the prune() job deletes token account history (via TokenAccountHistoryRepository.prune()) without the token account holder's consent, removing the historical record of token association authorizations that the account holder never approved for deletion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/retention/RetentionJob.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: prune()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the prune() job's log.info() at line 86 reveals the exact endTimestamp used for deletion, allowing an attacker monitoring logs to confirm which transaction signatures were deleted without owner consent and use that information to craft repudiation attacks?
